title: magnolia obovata var. soulangeana
intro: Magnolia obovata var. soulangeana, also known as the soulangeana magnolia, is a deciduous tree species native to China and Southeast Asia, characterized by its showy, white flowers with a purple center. The bark and flowers of the magnolia obovata var. soulangeana have been traditionally used in herbal medicine, with reported medicinal properties including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral effects. The health benefits of this plant are said to include relief from fever, sore throats, and respiratory issues, as well as potential antioxidant and immunomodulatory effects. In traditional herbal preparations, the bark and flowers of the magnolia obovata var. soulangeana are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
